items.title: Community decries government 'arrogance' over fracking bill

children.content: Producers on South Australia's Limestone Coast say they are stunned by a government proposal to lift the fracking ban in the region.

articles.title: Labor's 'jaw dropping' fracking decision wedges One Nation at first opportunity

children.content: The Malinauskas government's push to end the fracking moratorium in South East SA is a brutal welcome to politics for a new One Nation MP, as Labor fights an environmental war against both the left and right.

image.altText: Two side-by-side photos of two different men

articles.title: 'The premier can frack off': Government plans to remove fracking ban in SA's South East

children.content: The South Australian government is planning to lift a ban on fracking in the state's South East amid reports of increasing risks to gas supply in southern parts of the nation.

image.altText: Peter Malinauskas looks serious mid-speech

gallery.altText: A sign reading No fracking with our water, next to a man and woman sitting on a bench

thumbnail.altText: A sign reading No fracking with our water, next to a man and woman sitting on a bench

items.title: Protesters say they are in chains at North Adelaide Golf Course redevelopment

children.content: A protester has told 891 ABC Adelaide she has chained herself to a gate on War Memorial Drive and that four others have chained themselves to machinery inside the North Adelaide Golf Course.

image.altText: A line of protesters stand next to temporary fencing holding placards

items.title: Sensory-friendly show breaks 'theatre etiquette' to make audience feel safer

children.content: The State Theatre Company South Australia has introduced a sensory-friendly performance for a broader range of audience members including neurodivergent people.

image.altText: A woman and another person feels different fabric strips that makes up a jacket

items.title: Fire at North Adelaide Golf Course suspicious, police say

children.content: Police are investigating a suspicious fire that destroyed a temporary structure at the course amid a controversial redevelopment. In a separate incident, work was briefly halted when the perimeter fence was breached, and two people were later arrested for alleged trespassing.

articles.title: Protesters demand 'stop the chop' at rally opposing North Adelaide golf project

children.content: More than 2,000 people gathered at Parliament House in Adelaide to protest against the chopping down of trees to make way for the North Adelaide Golf Course redevelopment.

image.altText: Hundreds of protesters gather on the steps of South Australia's parliament.

gallery.altText: A temporary building at a building site gutted by fire

thumbnail.altText: A temporary building at a building site gutted by fire

items.title: Police search abandoned mine shafts for missing woman Trisha Graf

children.content: Major Crime investigators have packed up after focusing on outback SA mine shafts in their search for 41-year-old Trisha Graf, who went missing last year.

image.altText: A policeman has his back turned. The vast outback is in the background.

items.title: Gun shop owners grapple with uncertainty amid firearm reform

children.content: A lack of clarity on the South Australian government's position on gun control reform has caused a downturn in revenue for retailers.

image.altText: A man stands at a shop counter, guns are behind him

items.title: Notorious paedophile's lawyer says indefinite jail would be an 'abuse of process'

children.content: A lawyer for paedophile former magistrate Peter Liddy asks a court to permanently stay an application to keep his client behind bars, but a lawyer for the SA Attorney-General says Liddy continues to deny his crimes.

image.altText: Peter Liddy

items.title: Whyalla steelworks remains 'fiscal risk' in budget as SA waits for freight bypass funding

children.content: The federal government's ongoing financial support for the Whyalla steelworks remains a "fiscal risk" to the budget. Meanwhile, the South Australian government continues to hold out for more Commonwealth funding for a major road project.

image.altText: A view from near the blast furnace of the Whyalla steelworks.

items.title: Delta Goodrem reaches Eurovision grand final after fiery performance

children.content: Delta Goodrem has performed her pop ballad Eclipse at Eurovision in Vienna, the 11th time Australia has appeared in the musical extravaganza.

image.altText: Delta Goodrem singing.

gallery.altText: Delta Goodrem with her arms outstretched standing behind a golden piano.

thumbnail.altText: Delta Goodrem with her arms outstretched standing behind a golden piano.

items.title: Omega-3 testing to be introduced in SA to prevent preterm birth

children.content: The South Australian government has announced that pregnant women will be able to access free omega-3 blood tests from next month.

image.altText: A mother holds her baby in a lab

items.title: Woman stole parrot from pet shop by hiding it under top, police allege

children.content: South Australian police swoop on the alleged perpetrator of a parrot theft at a suburban Adelaide pet shop, but say the bird has not been found.

image.altText: A parrot police say was stolen.

items.title: Taylor 'huffing and puffing on the dog whistle': Labor criticises budget reply

children.content: Health Minister Mark Butler says the group Australia repatriated from the hantavirus cruise ship are in "good shape" ahead of their arrival in Perth today. Follow live.

image.altText: man in blue suit and green tie with still face looking straight ahead

items.title: Soon after John quit the Liberal Party, the strange phone calls started

children.content: When John Ternel quit the Victorian Liberal Party this week the party mistakenly sent an email to members saying he had died. Mr Ternel says the ordeal confirmed to him that the Victorian Liberals are in shambles.

image.altText: A man in a light business shirt and dark jacket looks into camera, with office buildings behind him.

items.title: The food, music and praise served up at the Trump-Xi state banquet

children.content: Guests dined on Beijing roast duck and tiramisu while the People's Liberation Army band performed some classic hits.

articles.title: The key takeaways from Trump and Xi's meeting in China

children.content: From a red carpet welcome to a high-profile tech entourage and a pointed reference to Taiwan, here are the main takeaways from Donald Trump's meeting with Xi Jinping in China.

image.altText: Donald Trump and Xi Jinping shake hands while posing for photos.

articles.title: Who is in Trump's entourage of billionaires and executives in Beijing?

children.content: US President Donald Trump has brought a delegation of business executives for his visit to Beijing. Here's why they made the trans-Pacific trip.

image.altText: Trump and Xi walk along a red carpet in front of rows of delegates standing on stone stairs.

items.title: ASX set to open higher as Wall Street rallies with new records

children.content: The ASX is set to open higher in morning trading after rallies on Wall Street. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: An ASX board shot by John Gunn in Deccember 2022.

items.title: 'Productive' first day of Israel and Lebanon talks as Trump discusses Iran with Xi

children.content: Israel and Lebanon have reported a productive first day of talks in Washington as the deadline for the ceasefire between the two countries approaches. Follow live.

image.altText: Emergency responders gather around a burning vehicle at the scene of an Israeli airstrike

items.title: Sam Kerr to leave Chelsea after six years at London club

image.altText: Sam Kerr claps after helping Chelsea defeat Leicester in the WSL.

children.content: The Matildas star has spent over six years playing for the London-based team.

items.title: How the wisdom of experience won the State of Origin series for New South Wales

children.content: New South Wales series win over Queensland proves that sometimes, like anything else, practice makes perfect in State of Origin football.

image.altText: A team celebrates winning a rugby league match

items.title: Overdose at Crown Melbourne every two weeks, data shows

children.content: Paramedics have attended more than 50 suspected overdoses at Crown Melbourne in the past two years, data released exclusively to the ABC shows.

image.altText: One of the main entrances to Crown Casino in Melbourne.

items.title: Twenty years since greatest live TV blunder when BBC interviewed wrong guy

children.content: Guy Gomo reflects on his infamous live TV mix-up, which has been immortalised as one of the greatest TV blunders of all time.

something
await you.

items.title: Ten still in hospital after bus rollover in north Queensland

children.content: Queensland police have described a "confronting scene" at a bus crash on a remote stretch of the Bruce Highway on Thursday afternoon.

image.altText: A green bus its on its side on a patch of grass.

items.title: Archaeologists restoring oldest colonial-era boat ever found in Australia

children.content: Maritime archaeologists are working to restore the oldest colonial-era boat ever found in Australia, discovered during the construction of the Barangaroo metro station in 2018.

image.altText: Archaeologists wearing high-vis and protective gear working together to excavate colonial-era boat.

gallery.altText: A team of eight in high-vis working to excavate boat remains at a build site, the CBD in the background.

thumbnail.altText: A team of eight in high-vis working to excavate boat remains at a build site, the CBD in the background.

items.title: Indigenous housing collapse puts families at risk of homelessness

children.content: Tenants in some Mount Isa homes have endured years without repairs, hot water or proper leases and now one corporation's failures could leave them homeless.

image.altText: Kerry Major stand in front of her house, with a hand on her mail box

items.title: 'Mega' gas-fired power stations could be the new normal, analyst says

children.content: Energy Australia says the proposed 1.4-gigawatt gas-fired power plant would be critical to the energy transition by supplying reliable back-up power, but analysts have flagged supply and economic issues.

image.altText: artsits impression of a power station with a gas power plant in the background, in a rural setting

items.title: Abandoned base's toxic waste clean-up deadline rejected

children.content: The Australian government has officially ruled out cleaning up Antarctica's contaminated Wilkes Station before the end of the decade, as previously recommended by a federal parliamentary committee.

gallery.altText: Images of an abandoned station in the icy and snowy Antarctic wilderness.

thumbnail.altText: Images of an abandoned station in the icy and snowy Antarctic wilderness.

items.title: Sexual abuse, a thwarted Olympic dream, and the sister who uncovered the truth

children.content: Ian Walker was one of the most promising athletes in the country until his life started to unravel, and the search for the truth in his tragic story has finally come to light.

image.altText: An older image of three young siblings arm in arm smiling at each other.

items.title: Research links seismic testing to decreased commercial fish catch

children.content: New research links seismic testing used for oil and gas exploration with decreases in commercial fish catch rates in Victoria's east.

items.title: Shed weight, go slow and pump up your tyres to save on fuel

items.title: Seven dead, scores missing in Kyiv as Russia continues onslaught

children.content: Russia launches hundreds of drones at Ukraine in the third straight day of widespread attacks on targets across the country.

image.altText: A woman holding her hands to her face and crying as she looks off camera.
